edibility: Inedible — extremely bitter and peppery; not toxic but unpalatable; harvested only for natural dye (yellows/greens)
taste: Sharply bitter, peppery, acrid — too unpleasant to swallow
search: Where/when to find — late summer/autumn, mossy conifer forests with pine/spruce, bright red 'sap' droplets on young white caps; look-alikes (other Hydnellum spp.)

toxicity.symptoms: N/A — not toxic, just inedibly bitter; accumulates cesium-137 in fallout zones
